violations.text: The cutting boards in the kitchen are very scratched and scored. Cutting blocks and boards that are subject to scratching and scoring shall be resurfaced if they can no longer be effectively cleaned and sanitized, or discarded if they are not capable of being resurfaced. New Violation. Correct By: 04/15/2026

The presence of pests is not controlled as evidenced by droppings in the cafetria and storage rooms on the side of the kitchen. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. Establishment increase pest control. Establishment must clean any and all droppings. Establishment must have full Evalution of building conducted by licensed pest control. Pest control reports must be available to inspector for Re-Inspection New Violation. Correct By: 11/01/2025
The handle on the one door freezer was observed broken. Equipment must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 11/01/2025
Establishment has a Serv-Safe employee not registered with Rhode Island Department of Health. New Violation. Correct By: 02/21/2024

Soft cheese located at the 2-door reach-in was observed with a date to exceed the 7 days limit for ready to eat food. (1/22/2024). Food item was voluntarily disposed of. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/07/2024

The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety at all times when time temperature control for safety (TCS) food is being prepared, handled, and/or served. New Violation. Correct By: 05/08/2023

Deli meat was observed not marked with the date of preparation. Ready-to-eat time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods prepared in the establishment and held for more than 24 hours must be marked with the date of preparation. New Violation. Correct By: 04/24/2023

Staff were observed not wearing hair restraints. Food employees must wear hair restraints such as hats, hair coverings, or nets that are worn to effectively keep their hair from contacting exposed food, clean equipment/utensils, linens, and unwrapped single-service and single-use articles. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/24/2023
